novel_features.name: Snapshot fanout across 7 sources
novel_features.description: Look up a company across SEC Form D, GitHub, Hacker News, Companies House, YC, Wikidata, and DNS in one command — rendered as a unified terminal summary in seconds.
novel_features.rationale: Requires parallel coordination across 7 independent APIs with different auth, rate limits, and response shapes — no single source can answer this and no existing CLI fans across them.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When you need to evaluate a startup quickly, this is the one command that tells you whether they raised, are shipping code, are talked about, and are legitimate — without 8 browser tabs.

novel_features.name: SEC Form D fundraising lookup
novel_features.description: See structured SEC Form D filings for a US private company — offering amount, filing date, exemption claimed (Reg D 506(b) vs 506(c)), related entities, and state of incorporation.
novel_features.rationale: Most US startups raising priced rounds file Form D with the SEC. Crunchbase Free hides this; Crunchbase Pro is thousands/year. We extract directly from the SEC's free public filings.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs to verify a US startup's actual fundraising history, this is the single most reliable free signal. Avoid agents quoting Crunchbase Free's empty 'undisclosed' rows.

novel_features.name: Cross-source signal consistency check
novel_features.description: Surface suspicious cross-source patterns. Example: 'Form D says raised $5M in 2024 but GitHub org has 0 commits since 2022' or 'YC entry says Active but website domain expired'.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining facts across SEC, GitHub, YC, and DNS that no single source has. Local SQLite store makes the joins cheap.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When deciding whether to engage with a startup, this command flags zombie companies and stale fundraising stories without you having to open every tab.

novel_features.name: Funding cadence over time
novel_features.description: Time series of Form D filings for a company across years — shows fundraising cadence and gaps. Useful for spotting 'they haven't raised since 2022' silently.
novel_features.rationale: Form D filings paginated by date is painful via the SEC web UI. Pre-extracted into the local store, this becomes a one-line query.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to summarize a company's fundraising arc, not just the latest round.

novel_features.name: FTS search across synced companies
novel_features.description: Search the YC directory by free text + --batch and --industry filters. Free-text matches against name, one-liner description, industry, and location.
novel_features.rationale: The local SQLite store turns the CLI into a personal research database. No other tool lets you SQL/FTS your own research history offline.
novel_features.why_it_matters: An agent tasked with 'find a YC fintech with "agent" in the description' has a one-shot query rather than scrolling the YC directory.

novel_features.name: Side-by-side comparison
novel_features.description: Two snapshots aligned column-by-column for direct comparison. Free in this CLI; paid feature elsewhere.
novel_features.rationale: Crunchbase compare is paid-tier; we get it free because we already have all the data locally after sync.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When evaluating two competing startups, this is the one-shot comparison that doesn't require flipping between tabs.

novel_features.name: Form D related-person graph
novel_features.description: Show every Form D filing that names a given person (officer, large holder). Reveals serial founders, repeat advisors, prolific investors.
novel_features.rationale: Form D filings list related persons; aggregating by person across filings is something neither Crunchbase nor PitchBook free tiers expose.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs to map who's behind a constellation of startups, or verify a founder's actual filing history.

narrative.headline: Look up startups across SEC Form D, GitHub, Hacker News, Companies House, YC, and Wikidata in one command — including the SEC fundraising data hidden behind paid Crunchbase tiers.
narrative.value_prop: company-goat-pp-cli fans out across seven free authoritative sources to research small and midsize startups in seconds. The killer feature is SEC Form D extraction: most US startups raising priced rounds file Form D with the SEC, and almost nobody outside finance knows this is queryable for free. After one snapshot, you know whether they raised, are shipping code, are talked about on HN, and are a real legal entity — without eight browser tabs.
narrative.auth_narrative: No API keys required for the killer feature. Optional GITHUB_TOKEN raises engineering-signal rate limits from 60/hr to 5000/hr (works with `gh auth token`). Optional COMPANIES_HOUSE_API_KEY enables UK legal entity lookup; without it, `legal --uk` prints setup instructions and other commands work normally.

quickstart.comment: The headline command — fan out across all 7 sources for a company you know exists.
The killer feature — pull SEC Form D filings showing offering amount, date, and exemption claimed.
Engineering signal as JSON — feed to jq or pipe into another tool.
Side-by-side compare two startups; great for due diligence.
Cross-source consistency check — flags zombies and stale fundraising stories.

troubleshoots.symptom: SEC EDGAR returns 403 or rate-limit error
troubleshoots.fix: Set a contact email in config: `company-goat-pp-cli config set sec.user_agent_email you@example.com`. SEC requires identifying your User-Agent under fair-access policy.
Run `gh auth login` so GITHUB_TOKEN is set, or `export GITHUB_TOKEN=ghp_...`. Raises limit from 60/hr to 5000/hr.
Register at developer.companieshouse.gov.uk (free), create a REST application, then `export COMPANIES_HOUSE_API_KEY=...`.
Pass --domain explicitly: `company-goat-pp-cli snapshot --domain anthropic.com`. The name resolution couldn't disambiguate.

troubleshoots.symptom: Multiple candidates returned for a name
troubleshoots.fix: Rerun with `--pick N` (1-indexed) or `--domain` to disambiguate. Exit code 2 indicates disambiguation needed.

narrative.when_to_use: Use Company GOAT when an agent or human needs structured company-research data on a small or midsize startup — fundraising, engineering signal, mention timeline, legal entity, domain age — without paying for Crunchbase Pro. Best for one-company-at-a-time deep research. Not for bulk discovery (sources don't scale to thousands of queries) and not for global private-market funding-graph queries (no source has that for free).

recipes.title: Quick due diligence on a US startup
recipes.explanation: Pull only the three most decision-relevant sections. --select avoids burning context on Wikidata or HN data you don't need.

recipes.title: Find every SEC filing for a founder
recipes.explanation: Returns every Form D filing where this person is named — useful for mapping serial founders and repeat investors.

recipes.title: Compare two competing startups
recipes.explanation: Aligns snapshots side-by-side; agent-readable. Use for tradeoff analysis.

recipes.title: Build a research database
recipes.command: company-goat-pp-cli sync stripe ramp brex anthropic perplexity && company-goat-pp-cli search 'series b'
recipes.explanation: Sync multiple companies into local SQLite, then FTS-search across them. Your private research store.

recipes.title: Surface suspicious patterns
recipes.explanation: Cross-source consistency check — flags cases like 'raised $X in 2024 but GitHub silent since 2022' that no single source would catch.
